title: The Drying Rack Symphony
text_zh: 刮大风的日子，整个小区的晾衣架都在响。金属杆碰在一起的声音，叮叮叮，像风铃一样。有的衣服被吹得飞了起来，像挣脱了绳子的风筝。我站在阳台上看了一会儿，发现每个人晾衣服的方式都不一样：有人把衣服用夹子夹得紧紧的，大风也吹不掉；有人只是随便搭在绳子上，一有风就掉了下来。楼下有个阿姨正在追一件被风吹到草地上的床单，跑了好远才追到。她把床单抱在怀里，气喘吁吁地笑着。风停了以后，一切恢复了安静。但是我注意到有一件红色的毛衣挂在了三楼的空调外机上，不知道是谁家的。它在那里挂了三天，才被人用一根长竹竿够下来。
text_pinyin: Guā dà fēng de rìzi, zhěnggè xiǎoqū de liàng yī jià dōu zài xiǎng. Jīnshǔ gǎn pèng zài yìqǐ de shēngyīn, dīng dīng dīng, xiàng fēnglíng yíyàng. Yǒude yīfu bèi chuī de fēi le qǐlái, xiàng zhēngtuō le shéngzi de fēngzhēng. Wǒ zhàn zài yángtái shàng kàn le yíhuìr, fāxiàn měi gè rén liàng yīfu de fāngshì dōu bù yíyàng: yǒu rén bǎ yīfu yòng jiāzi jiā de jǐnjǐn de, dà fēng yě chuī bu diào; yǒu rén zhǐ shì suíbiàn dā zài shéngzi shàng, yì yǒu fēng jiù diào le xiàlái. Lóu xià yǒu ge āyí zhèngzài zhuī yí jiàn bèi fēng chuī dào cǎodì shàng de chuángdān, pǎo le hǎo yuǎn cái zhuī dào. Tā bǎ chuángdān bào zài huái lǐ, qìchuǎn-xūxū de xiàozhe. Fēng tíng le yǐhòu, yíqiè huīfù le ānjìng. Dànshì wǒ zhùyì dào yǒu yí jiàn hóng sè de máoyī guà zài le sān lóu de kōngtiáo wàijī shàng, bù zhīdào shì shéi jiā de. Tā zài nàlǐ guà le sān tiān, cái bèi rén yòng yì gēn cháng zhúgān gòu xiàlái.
text_en: On windy days, all the drying racks in the compound ring out. Metal rods clanking together — ding, ding, ding — like wind chimes. Some clothes get blown into the air, like kites breaking free of their strings. I stood on the balcony watching and noticed everyone hangs laundry differently: some clip clothes on tightly with pegs, and even strong wind can't blow them off; others just drape things casually on the line, and they fall at the first breeze. Downstairs, an auntie was chasing a bedsheet the wind blew onto the lawn, running quite far before catching it. She hugged it to her chest, laughing and out of breath. After the wind died down, everything returned to quiet. But I noticed a red sweater had landed on the third floor's outdoor AC unit — who knows whose it was. It hung there for three days before someone reached it down with a long bamboo pole.

questions.q_zh: 大风天小区里什么在响？
questions.q_en: What's ringing in the compound on windy days?

questions.q_zh: 楼下的阿姨在追什么？
questions.q_en: What was the auntie downstairs chasing?

options.pinyin: yí jiàn bèi fēng chuī zǒu de chuángdān
options.text_en: a bedsheet blown away by wind

questions.q_zh: 红色的毛衣最后怎么拿下来的？
questions.q_en: How was the red sweater finally retrieved?

options.pinyin: fēng bǎ tā chuī xiàlái le
yòng cháng zhúgān gòu xiàlái de
options.text_en: reached down with a long bamboo pole

title: The Grandfather Clock
text_zh: 客厅里有一个老座钟，是爷爷留下来的。它每到整点就会「当——当——」地响，几点就响几下。小时候我被这个声音吵得睡不着，现在反而离不开它了。有一次座钟坏了，家里突然安静得不对劲，我才意识到它的声音已经变成了家的一部分。爸爸找了一个修钟的师傅来，师傅说这种钟的零件很难找了，要花不少时间。在钟停了的那两个星期里，每到整点我都会习惯性地等那个声音，但是什么也没有。等钟修好重新响起来的那一刻，全家人都笑了。妈妈说：「这个钟比你们任何一个人的年纪都大，它是这个家里最老的成员。」我想她说得对——这个座钟不只是在报时，它在给一个家数着年龄。
text_pinyin: Kètīng lǐ yǒu yí gè lǎo zuòzhōng, shì yéye liú xiàlái de. Tā měi dào zhěng diǎn jiù huì「dāng——dāng——」de xiǎng, jǐ diǎn jiù xiǎng jǐ xià. Xiǎo shíhou wǒ bèi zhège shēngyīn chǎo de shuì bu zháo, xiànzài fǎn'ér lí bu kāi tā le. Yǒu yí cì zuòzhōng huài le, jiā lǐ tūrán ānjìng de bú duìjìn, wǒ cái yìshi dào tā de shēngyīn yǐjīng biàn chéng le jiā de yí bùfen. Bàba zhǎo le yí gè xiū zhōng de shīfu lái, shīfu shuō zhè zhǒng zhōng de língjiàn hěn nán zhǎo le, yào huā bù shǎo shíjiān. Zài zhōng tíng le de nà liǎng gè xīngqī lǐ, měi dào zhěng diǎn wǒ dōu huì xíguànxìng de děng nàge shēngyīn, dànshì shénme yě méiyǒu. Děng zhōng xiū hǎo chóngxīn xiǎng qǐlái de nà yí kè, quán jiā rén dōu xiào le. Māma shuō: 「Zhège zhōng bǐ nǐmen rènhé yí gè rén de niánjì dōu dà, tā shì zhège jiā lǐ zuì lǎo de chéngyuán.」 Wǒ xiǎng tā shuō de duì——zhège zuòzhōng bù zhǐ shì zài bàoshí, tā zài gěi yí gè jiā shùzhe niánlíng.
text_en: In the living room there's an old grandfather clock, left by my grandfather. On every hour it chimes — dong, dong — as many times as the hour. As a child, the sound kept me from sleeping; now I can't do without it. Once the clock broke, and the house went strangely quiet — only then did I realize its sound had become part of the home. Dad found a clock repairman, who said parts for this kind of clock are hard to find and would take a while. During the two weeks it was stopped, I'd instinctively wait for the chime at each hour, but nothing came. The moment the repaired clock chimed again, the whole family smiled. Mom said: 'This clock is older than any of you — it's the oldest member of this family.' I think she's right. This clock isn't just keeping time; it's counting a family's years.

questions.q_zh: 座钟是谁留下来的？
questions.q_en: Who left the clock behind?

questions.q_zh: 座钟坏了以后「我」有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel when the clock broke?

options.text: 终于可以安静地睡觉了
options.pinyin: zhōngyú kěyǐ ānjìng de shuìjiào le
jiā lǐ ānjìng de bú duìjìn

questions.q_zh: 妈妈说座钟是家里的什么？
questions.q_en: What does Mom call the clock in the family?

title: The Shared Bicycle Graveyard
text_zh: 在城市的郊区，有一个空地上堆满了成千上万辆共享单车。黄色的、蓝色的、绿色的，堆成了一座小山。这些车大部分都是坏了的——有的没有座椅，有的轮子歪了，有的二维码被刮掉了。从远处看，它们像一幅色彩鲜艳的抽象画。从近处看，却让人觉得有点可惜。几年前，共享单车刚出来的时候，大家都觉得特别方便，走到哪里都能骑。但是后来太多的公司一起做，车越来越多，路上到处都是。很多车被随便扔在路边、河里、甚至树上。最后很多公司倒闭了，留下了这些没人管的车。这个「自行车坟场」让我想到：不管多好的东西，如果没有节制地发展，最后都可能变成一种浪费。
text_pinyin: Zài chéngshì de jiāoqū, yǒu yí gè kòngdì shàng duī mǎn le chéng qiān shàng wàn liàng gòngxiǎng dānchē. Huáng sè de, lán sè de, lǜ sè de, duī chéng le yí zuò xiǎo shān. Zhèxiē chē dà bùfen dōu shì huài le de——yǒude méiyǒu zuòyǐ, yǒude lúnzi wāi le, yǒude èrwéimǎ bèi guā diào le. Cóng yuǎnchù kàn, tāmen xiàng yì fú sècǎi xiānyàn de chōuxiàng huà. Cóng jìnchù kàn, què ràng rén juéde yǒudiǎn kěxī. Jǐ nián qián, gòngxiǎng dānchē gāng chūlái de shíhou, dàjiā dōu juéde tèbié fāngbiàn, zǒu dào nǎlǐ dōu néng qí. Dànshì hòulái tài duō de gōngsī yìqǐ zuò, chē yuè lái yuè duō, lù shàng dàochù dōu shì. Hěn duō chē bèi suíbiàn rēng zài lùbiān, hé lǐ, shènzhì shù shàng. Zuìhòu hěn duō gōngsī dǎobì le, liú xià le zhèxiē méi rén guǎn de chē. Zhège「zìxíngchē fénchǎng」ràng wǒ xiǎng dào: bùguǎn duō hǎo de dōngxi, rúguǒ méiyǒu jiézhì de fāzhǎn, zuìhòu dōu kěnéng biàn chéng yì zhǒng làngfèi.
text_en: On the outskirts of the city, an empty lot is piled high with thousands upon thousands of shared bicycles. Yellow, blue, green — stacked into a small mountain. Most of these bikes are broken: some missing seats, some with bent wheels, some with QR codes scraped off. From a distance they look like a vivid abstract painting. Up close, they feel a bit sad. A few years ago when shared bikes first appeared, everyone thought they were incredibly convenient — you could ride one anywhere. But then too many companies jumped in, bikes multiplied endlessly, cluttering streets everywhere. Many were carelessly tossed on roadsides, in rivers, even in trees. Eventually many companies went bankrupt, leaving these ownerless bikes behind. This 'bicycle graveyard' made me think: no matter how good something is, if it grows without restraint, it can end up as waste.

questions.q_zh: 空地上的共享单车是什么样的？
questions.q_en: What are the shared bikes on the lot like?

options.pinyin: dà bùfen shì huài le de

questions.q_zh: 共享单车为什么变成了这样？
questions.q_en: Why did the shared bikes end up like this?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi dàjiā bù xǐhuan qí chē

options.text: 因为太多公司一起做，最后很多倒闭了
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tài duō gōngsī yìqǐ zuò, zuìhòu hěn duō dǎobì le
options.text_en: too many companies competed, many went bankrupt

options.pinyin: yīnwèi zhèngfǔ bú ràng qí le
yīnwèi chē de zhìliàng tài chà

questions.q_en: What does 'I' reflect on from this?

options.text: 没有节制的发展可能变成浪费
options.pinyin: méiyǒu jiézhì de fāzhǎn kěnéng biàn chéng làngfèi

options.text_en: shouldn't ride bikes in the future

title: The Paper Airplane
text_zh: 今天下午我在阳台上看书的时候，一架纸飞机突然从天上飞了下来，落在了我的腿上。纸飞机是用作业纸折的，上面还能看到几道数学题。我把纸飞机打开一看，反面写着一句话：「谁捡到了我的飞机，请扔回来。——五楼的小明。」我笑了笑，重新把纸折好，用力往上扔了回去。但是风太大了，飞机被吹到了隔壁楼的阳台上。我正不知道怎么办的时候，又一架纸飞机飞了下来。上面写着：「上一架没有回来，再试一次。」我这次站起来，对准五楼的方向使劲一扔。过了几秒钟，我听到楼上传来一声欢呼：「接住了！」虽然我不认识五楼的小明，但是这几分钟的「纸飞机对话」让我的下午变得有趣多了。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān xiàwǔ wǒ zài yángtái shàng kàn shū de shíhou, yí jià zhǐ fēijī tūrán cóng tiān shàng fēi le xiàlái, luò zài le wǒ de tuǐ shàng. Zhǐ fēijī shì yòng zuòyè zhǐ zhé de, shàngmiàn hái néng kàn dào jǐ dào shùxué tí. Wǒ bǎ zhǐ fēijī dǎkāi yí kàn, fǎnmiàn xiězhe yí jù huà: 「Shéi jiǎn dào le wǒ de fēijī, qǐng rēng huílái.——wǔ lóu de Xiǎo Míng.」 Wǒ xiào le xiào, chóngxīn bǎ zhǐ zhé hǎo, yònglì wǎng shàng rēng le huíqù. Dànshì fēng tài dà le, fēijī bèi chuī dào le gébì lóu de yángtái shàng. Wǒ zhèng bù zhīdào zěnme bàn de shíhou, yòu yí jià zhǐ fēijī fēi le xiàlái. Shàngmiàn xiězhe: 「Shàng yí jià méiyǒu huílái, zài shì yí cì.」 Wǒ zhè cì zhàn qǐlái, duìzhǔn wǔ lóu de fāngxiàng shǐjìn yì rēng. Guò le jǐ miǎozhōng, wǒ tīng dào lóu shàng chuán lái yì shēng huānhū: 「Jiē zhù le!」 Suīrán wǒ bú rènshi wǔ lóu de Xiǎo Míng, dànshì zhè jǐ fēnzhōng de「zhǐ fēijī duìhuà」ràng wǒ de xiàwǔ biàn de yǒuqù duō le.
text_en: This afternoon while reading on the balcony, a paper airplane suddenly flew down from above and landed on my lap. It was folded from homework paper — you could still see some math problems on it. I unfolded it and found a message on the back: 'Whoever finds my airplane, please throw it back. — Xiao Ming from the fifth floor.' I smiled, refolded the paper, and threw it back up hard. But the wind was too strong and blew it onto the neighboring building's balcony. Just as I was wondering what to do, another paper airplane flew down. It read: 'The last one didn't come back. Trying again.' This time I stood up, aimed at the fifth floor, and threw with all my strength. A few seconds later, I heard a cheer from upstairs: 'Caught it!' Although I don't know Xiao Ming from the fifth floor, these few minutes of 'paper airplane conversation' made my afternoon much more interesting.

questions.q_zh: 纸飞机是用什么纸折的？
questions.q_en: What paper was the airplane made from?

questions.q_zh: 第一次扔回去为什么没成功？
questions.q_en: Why did the first throw back fail?

options.text: 风太大了，被吹到隔壁楼了
options.pinyin: fēng tài dà le, bèi chuī dào gébì lóu le
options.text_en: wind was too strong, blown to neighboring building

questions.q_zh: 这件事让「我」觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did this make 'I' feel?

options.pinyin: xiàwǔ biàn de yǒuqù duō le

title: The Night Shift Nurse
text_zh: 我姐姐是一名护士，经常上夜班。她说夜班最难的不是工作本身，而是在半夜两三点的时候对抗困意。医院的夜晚和白天完全不一样：走廊的灯光调得很暗，护士站只有一盏小台灯亮着。大部分病人都睡着了，偶尔有人按呼叫铃，姐姐就马上跑过去看。她说最害怕听到的不是呼叫铃，而是半夜突然的安静——因为安静有时候意味着有病人出了问题。每次上完夜班回家，姐姐都会先洗一个热水澡，然后拉上窗帘，在白天睡觉。但是她说白天睡觉永远不如晚上睡得好，因为窗外总有阳光和声音提醒你现在是白天。虽然很辛苦，但她从来没有抱怨过。她说：「只要病人平平安安地过了一夜，我的夜班就是值得的。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiějie shì yì míng hùshi, jīngcháng shàng yèbān. Tā shuō yèbān zuì nán de búshì gōngzuò běnshēn, érshì zài bànyè liǎng sān diǎn de shíhou duìkàng kùnyì. Yīyuàn de yèwǎn hé báitiān wánquán bù yíyàng: zǒuláng de dēngguāng tiáo de hěn àn, hùshizhàn zhǐ yǒu yì zhǎn xiǎo táidēng liàngzhe. Dà bùfen bìngrén dōu shuìzháo le, ǒu'ěr yǒu rén àn hūjiào líng, jiějie jiù mǎshàng pǎo guòqù kàn. Tā shuō zuì hàipà tīng dào de búshì hūjiào líng, érshì bànyè tūrán de ānjìng——yīnwèi ānjìng yǒu shíhou yìwèizhe yǒu bìngrén chū le wèntí. Měi cì shàng wán yèbān huí jiā, jiějie dōu huì xiān xǐ yí gè rè shuǐ zǎo, ránhòu lā shàng chuānglián, zài báitiān shuìjiào. Dànshì tā shuō báitiān shuìjiào yǒngyuǎn bùrú wǎnshang shuì de hǎo, yīnwèi chuāng wài zǒng yǒu yángguāng hé shēngyīn tíxǐng nǐ xiànzài shì báitiān. Suīrán hěn xīnkǔ, dàn tā cónglái méiyǒu bàoyuàn guò. Tā shuō: 「Zhǐyào bìngrén píng píng ān ān de guò le yí yè, wǒ de yèbān jiù shì zhíde de.」
text_en: My sister is a nurse who often works night shifts. She says the hardest part isn't the work itself, but fighting sleepiness at two or three in the morning. The hospital at night is completely different from the day: hallway lights are dimmed, and only a small desk lamp glows at the nurses' station. Most patients are asleep; occasionally someone presses the call button, and she rushes over immediately. She says what she fears hearing most isn't the call button, but sudden silence in the middle of the night — because silence sometimes means a patient has had a problem. After every night shift, she goes home, takes a hot bath, draws the curtains, and sleeps during the day. But she says daytime sleep is never as good as nighttime sleep, because sunlight and sounds outside always remind you it's daytime. Though it's very hard, she's never complained. She says: 'As long as the patients get through the night safely, my night shift is worth it.'

questions.q_zh: 夜班最难的是什么？
questions.q_en: What's the hardest part of the night shift?

options.pinyin: bànyè liǎng sān diǎn duìkàng kùnyì

questions.q_zh: 姐姐最害怕听到什么？
questions.q_en: What is the sister most afraid of hearing?

questions.q_zh: 姐姐觉得什么时候夜班是值得的？
questions.q_en: When does the sister think the night shift is worth it?

options.pinyin: bìngrén píng'ān de guò le yí yè
options.text_en: patients get through the night safely

title: The Extra Coffee
text_zh: 最近一个月，每个星期一早上我到办公室的时候，桌子上都会多出一杯咖啡。咖啡是热的，杯子上贴着一张纸条，写着「星期一快乐」。我问了办公室所有的同事，没有人承认是自己放的。我检查了一下咖啡，是我最喜欢的那种——冰美式，但是用保温杯装的，所以还是热的。这说明放咖啡的人知道我的喜好。我试过提前半个小时来，但是咖啡已经在那里了。我又试过来得更早，结果发现咖啡是在我到达之前五分钟放的——因为杯子还烫手。这个「咖啡侦探」游戏玩了一个月以后，我终于在一个星期一的早上，蹲在走廊的拐角处等到了答案：是前台的小张。她说：「因为你每个星期一看起来都很累，我想让你的一周有一个好的开始。」
text_pinyin: Zuìjìn yí gè yuè, měi gè xīngqīyī zǎoshang wǒ dào bàngōngshì de shíhou, zhuōzi shàng dōu huì duō chū yì bēi kāfēi. Kāfēi shì rè de, bēizi shàng tiēzhe yì zhāng zhǐtiáo, xiězhe「Xīngqīyī kuàilè」. Wǒ wèn le bàngōngshì suǒyǒu de tóngshì, méiyǒu rén chéngrèn shì zìjǐ fàng de. Wǒ jiǎnchá le yíxià kāfēi, shì wǒ zuì xǐhuan de nà zhǒng——bīng Měishì, dànshì yòng bǎowēn bēi zhuāng de, suǒyǐ háishi rè de. Zhè shuōmíng fàng kāfēi de rén zhīdào wǒ de xǐhào. Wǒ shì guò tíqián bàn gè xiǎoshí lái, dànshì kāfēi yǐjīng zài nàlǐ le. Wǒ yòu shì guò lái de gèng zǎo, jiéguǒ fāxiàn kāfēi shì zài wǒ dàodá zhīqián wǔ fēnzhōng fàng de——yīnwèi bēizi hái tàng shǒu. Zhège「kāfēi zhēntàn」yóuxì wán le yí gè yuè yǐhòu, wǒ zhōngyú zài yí gè xīngqīyī de zǎoshang, dūn zài zǒuláng de guǎijiǎo chù děng dào le dá'àn: shì qiántái de Xiǎo Zhāng. Tā shuō: 「Yīnwèi nǐ měi gè xīngqīyī kàn qǐlái dōu hěn lèi, wǒ xiǎng ràng nǐ de yì zhōu yǒu yí gè hǎo de kāishǐ.」
text_en: For the past month, every Monday morning when I arrive at the office, there's an extra cup of coffee on my desk. The coffee is hot, with a note on the cup reading 'Happy Monday.' I asked every colleague in the office — nobody admitted it. I examined the coffee: it's my favorite kind — iced Americano, but kept in a thermos so it's still hot. This means the person knows my preferences. I tried arriving half an hour early, but the coffee was already there. I tried coming even earlier and found the coffee was placed just five minutes before I arrived — because the cup was still burning hot. After a month of this 'coffee detective' game, I finally crouched around the corner of the hallway one Monday morning and got my answer: it was Xiao Zhang from reception. She said: 'Because you look so tired every Monday, I wanted your week to have a good start.'

questions.q_zh: 咖啡上面的纸条写着什么？
questions.q_en: What does the note on the coffee say?

questions.q_zh: 「我」最后怎么发现是谁放的咖啡？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' finally discover who placed the coffee?

options.pinyin: dūn zài zǒuláng guǎijiǎo děng dào le
options.text_en: crouched around the hallway corner and waited

questions.q_zh: 小张为什么送咖啡？
questions.q_en: Why did Xiao Zhang send coffee?

options.text: 因为「我」每个星期一看起来很累
options.pinyin: yīnwèi wǒ měi gè xīngqīyī kàn qǐlái hěn lèi
yīnwèi lǎobǎn ràng tā sòng de

title: The Photo I Can't Throw Away
text_zh: 整理房间的时候，我发现了一张小学毕业的集体照。照片已经发黄了，角上还有折痕。照片里的三十个孩子穿着白衬衫，站成三排，每个人都努力地笑着。我一个一个地看过去，发现很多人的名字我已经忘了。但是有几个人我还记得很清楚：坐在第一排最左边的是王小明，他总是考第一名；站在最后一排最高的是李强，他是班里最调皮的人。而站在中间、笑得最灿烂的那个女生，是我当时最好的朋友——但我们已经十几年没有联系了。我本来想把这张照片扔掉，因为它已经太旧了。但是拿在手里看了很久以后，我还是把它放回了抽屉里。有些东西你明明知道再也用不到了，但就是扔不掉。也许这就是回忆的重量。
text_pinyin: Zhěnglǐ fángjiān de shíhou, wǒ fāxiàn le yì zhāng xiǎoxué bìyè de jítǐ zhào. Zhàopiàn yǐjīng fā huáng le, jiǎo shàng hái yǒu zhéhén. Zhàopiàn lǐ de sānshí gè háizi chuānzhe bái chènshān, zhàn chéng sān pái, měi gè rén dōu nǔlì de xiàozhe. Wǒ yí gè yí gè de kàn guòqù, fāxiàn hěn duō rén de míngzi wǒ yǐjīng wàng le. Dànshì yǒu jǐ gè rén wǒ hái jìde hěn qīngchu: zuò zài dì yī pái zuì zuǒbiān de shì Wáng Xiǎomíng, tā zǒng shì kǎo dì yī míng; zhàn zài zuìhòu yì pái zuì gāo de shì Lǐ Qiáng, tā shì bān lǐ zuì tiáopí de rén. Ér zhàn zài zhōngjiān, xiào de zuì cànlàn de nàge nǚshēng, shì wǒ dāngshí zuì hǎo de péngyou——dàn wǒmen yǐjīng shí jǐ nián méiyǒu liánxì le. Wǒ běnlái xiǎng bǎ zhè zhāng zhàopiàn rēng diào, yīnwèi tā yǐjīng tài jiù le. Dànshì ná zài shǒu lǐ kàn le hěn jiǔ yǐhòu, wǒ háishi bǎ tā fàng huí le chōuti lǐ. Yǒuxiē dōngxi nǐ míngmíng zhīdào zài yě yòng bu dào le, dàn jiù shì rēng bu diào. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì huíyì de zhòngliàng.
text_en: While tidying my room, I found a group photo from elementary school graduation. The photo has yellowed, with creases at the corners. Thirty children in white shirts stand in three rows, each trying hard to smile. I looked at them one by one and found I'd forgotten many of their names. But a few I remember clearly: on the far left of the first row is Wang Xiaoming, who always came first in exams; the tallest in the back row is Li Qiang, the class troublemaker. And standing in the middle, smiling most brightly, is the girl who was my best friend at the time — but we haven't been in touch for over a decade. I originally wanted to throw this photo away because it's too old. But after holding it and looking at it for a long time, I put it back in the drawer. Some things you clearly know you'll never need again, yet you just can't throw them away. Perhaps that's the weight of memories.

questions.q_zh: 照片里的孩子穿着什么？
questions.q_en: What are the children wearing in the photo?

options.pinyin: gè zhǒng gè yàng de yīfu

questions.q_zh: 笑得最灿烂的女生是谁？
questions.q_en: Who is the girl smiling most brightly?

options.pinyin: wǒ dāngshí zuì hǎo de péngyou

questions.q_zh: 「我」最后把照片怎么了？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' finally do with the photo?

options.text_en: put it back in the drawer

title: The Lunch Box Exchange
text_zh: 办公室里有一个不成文的传统：每个星期五是「便当交换日」。愿意参加的同事自己带一份午饭来，然后随机跟另一个人交换。规则很简单——你不知道会吃到谁做的饭，对方也不知道会吃到你做的。这个活动是三年前一个同事提议的，刚开始只有四五个人参加，现在已经有十几个了。大家做的饭各种各样：有人擅长做面条，有人擅长做炒菜，有一个韩国同事每次都带泡菜炒饭。最有意思的是，你可以通过便当了解一个人的性格——做事认真的人连便当盒里的菜都摆得整整齐齐，随性的人就随便装在一个袋子里。有一次我交换到了一个特别好吃的红烧肉便当，从那以后我一直在找是谁做的，但是到现在还没找到。
text_pinyin: Bàngōngshì lǐ yǒu yí gè bù chéng wén de chuántǒng: měi gè xīngqīwǔ shì「biàndāng jiāohuàn rì」. Yuànyì cānjiā de tóngshì zìjǐ dài yí fèn wǔfàn lái, ránhòu suíjī gēn lìng yí gè rén jiāohuàn. Guīzé hěn jiǎndān——nǐ bù zhīdào huì chī dào shéi zuò de fàn, duìfāng yě bù zhīdào huì chī dào nǐ zuò de. Zhège huódòng shì sān nián qián yí gè tóngshì tíyì de, gāng kāishǐ zhǐ yǒu sì wǔ gè rén cānjiā, xiànzài yǐjīng yǒu shí jǐ gè le. Dàjiā zuò de fàn gè zhǒng gè yàng: yǒu rén shàncháng zuò miàntiáo, yǒu rén shàncháng zuò chǎo cài, yǒu yí gè Hánguó tóngshì měi cì dōu dài pàocài chǎo fàn. Zuì yǒu yìsi de shì, nǐ kěyǐ tōngguò biàndāng liǎojiě yí gè rén de xìnggé——zuò shì rènzhēn de rén lián biàndāng hé lǐ de cài dōu bǎi de zhěngzhěng-qíqí, suíxìng de rén jiù suíbiàn zhuāng zài yí gè dàizi lǐ. Yǒu yí cì wǒ jiāohuàn dào le yí gè tèbié hǎochī de hóngshāoròu biàndāng, cóng nà yǐhòu wǒ yìzhí zài zhǎo shì shéi zuò de, dànshì dào xiànzài hái méi zhǎo dào.
text_en: The office has an unwritten tradition: every Friday is 'lunch box exchange day.' Willing colleagues each bring a homemade lunch and randomly exchange with someone else. The rules are simple — you don't know whose food you'll eat, and they don't know they'll eat yours. This activity was proposed by a colleague three years ago. At first only four or five people joined; now there are over a dozen. Everyone makes different things: some are good at noodles, some at stir-fry. A Korean colleague always brings kimchi fried rice. The most interesting part is that you can understand a person's personality through their lunch box — meticulous people arrange food neatly even in the box, while easygoing people just stuff everything in a bag. Once I got a delicious braised pork lunch box. Since then I've been trying to find who made it, but I still haven't.

questions.q_zh: 「便当交换日」是每个星期几？
questions.q_en: What day is 'lunch box exchange day'?

questions.q_zh: 通过便当可以了解什么？
questions.q_en: What can you understand through the lunch boxes?

questions.q_zh: 「我」一直在找什么？
questions.q_en: What has 'I' been looking for?

options.text_en: the person who made the braised pork lunch

title: The Tree That Marks the Seasons
text_zh: 我办公室的窗户外面有一棵大银杏树。春天的时候，它先是光秃秃的，然后慢慢长出嫩绿色的小叶子。夏天叶子变得又大又密，像一把绿色的大伞。到了秋天，它是这条街上最好看的——所有的叶子在两三个星期内变成金黄色，然后一片一片地掉下来。冬天又变回了光秃秃的样子，只剩下黑色的树枝在天空下画出复杂的线条。我坐在同一个位置，每天看着同一棵树，但是它每天都在变。有时候我会忘了看窗外好几天，再一看的时候发现树已经完全不一样了。这让我想到，变化有时候是很慢的，慢到你几乎注意不到。但是只要你愿意停下来仔细看，就会发现世界每天都在不一样。这棵银杏树不需要说话，它只用颜色就能告诉你现在是哪个季节。
text_pinyin: Wǒ bàngōngshì de chuānghu wàimiàn yǒu yì kē dà yínxìng shù. Chūntiān de shíhou, tā xiān shì guāngtūtū de, ránhòu mànmàn zhǎng chū nèn lǜ sè de xiǎo yèzi. Xiàtiān yèzi biàn de yòu dà yòu mì, xiàng yì bǎ lǜ sè de dà sǎn. Dào le qiūtiān, tā shì zhè tiáo jiē shàng zuì hǎokàn de——suǒyǒu de yèzi zài liǎng sān gè xīngqī nèi biàn chéng jīnhuáng sè, ránhòu yí piàn yí piàn de diào xiàlái. Dōngtiān yòu biàn huí le guāngtūtū de yàngzi, zhǐ shèng xià hēi sè de shùzhī zài tiānkōng xià huà chū fùzá de xiàntiáo. Wǒ zuò zài tóng yí gè wèizhì, měi tiān kànzhe tóng yì kē shù, dànshì tā měi tiān dōu zài biàn. Yǒu shíhou wǒ huì wàng le kàn chuāng wài hǎo jǐ tiān, zài yí kàn de shíhou fāxiàn shù yǐjīng wánquán bù yíyàng le. Zhè ràng wǒ xiǎng dào, biànhuà yǒu shíhou shì hěn màn de, màn dào nǐ jīhū zhùyì bu dào. Dànshì zhǐyào nǐ yuànyì tíng xiàlái zǐxì kàn, jiù huì fāxiàn shìjiè měi tiān dōu zài bù yíyàng. Zhè kē yínxìng shù bù xūyào shuō huà, tā zhǐ yòng yánsè jiù néng gàosu nǐ xiànzài shì nǎge jìjié.
text_en: Outside my office window there's a large ginkgo tree. In spring, it starts bare, then slowly grows tender green leaves. In summer the leaves grow big and dense, like a green umbrella. In autumn, it's the most beautiful tree on the street — all its leaves turn golden yellow within two or three weeks, then fall one by one. In winter it returns to bareness, with only black branches drawing complex lines against the sky. I sit in the same seat looking at the same tree every day, yet it changes daily. Sometimes I forget to look out the window for several days, and when I look again, the tree is completely different. This reminds me that change is sometimes very slow — so slow you barely notice. But if you're willing to stop and look carefully, you'll find the world is different every day. This ginkgo tree doesn't need to speak; it tells you the season using only color.

questions.q_zh: 秋天的银杏树是什么颜色的？
questions.q_en: What color is the ginkgo tree in autumn?

questions.q_zh: 「我」好几天没看窗外以后发现了什么？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' find after not looking outside for days?

options.pinyin: shù yǐjīng wánquán bù yíyàng le
duō le yì kē xīn shù

questions.q_zh: 这棵银杏树用什么来告诉你季节？
questions.q_en: How does the ginkgo tree tell you the season?

title: The Overly Polite Neighbors
text_zh: 我和对面的邻居有一个奇怪的问题——我们都太客气了。每次在楼道里遇到，他说「你先走」，我说「不不不，你先走」，然后我们两个人就站在那里互相让了好几分钟。有一次，我们在电梯门口让了太久，电梯关上了又走了。上个星期他给我送了一盒月饼，我第二天给他送了一袋水果。然后他又给我送了一包茶叶。现在我觉得我还得再送他点什么东西，但是我不知道该送什么了。我太太说「你们两个应该签一个合同，规定一年只能互相送一次礼物。」我觉得她说得对，但是如果我去找他谈这件事，他一定会先请我进去喝茶，然后我又得带一个礼物去……这个循环什么时候才能结束？
text_pinyin: Wǒ hé duìmiàn de línjū yǒu yí gè qíguài de wèntí——wǒmen dōu tài kèqi le. Měi cì zài lóudào lǐ yù dào, tā shuō「nǐ xiān zǒu」, wǒ shuō「bù bù bù, nǐ xiān zǒu」, ránhòu wǒmen liǎng gè rén jiù zhàn zài nàlǐ hùxiāng ràng le hǎo jǐ fēnzhōng. Yǒu yí cì, wǒmen zài diàntī ménkǒu ràng le tài jiǔ, diàntī guān shàng le yòu zǒu le. Shàng gè xīngqī tā gěi wǒ sòng le yì hé yuèbǐng, wǒ dì èr tiān gěi tā sòng le yí dài shuǐguǒ. Ránhòu tā yòu gěi wǒ sòng le yì bāo cháyè. Xiànzài wǒ juéde wǒ hái děi zài sòng tā diǎn shénme dōngxi, dànshì wǒ bù zhīdào gāi sòng shénme le. Wǒ tàitai shuō「nǐmen liǎng gè yīnggāi qiān yí gè hétong, guīdìng yì nián zhǐ néng hùxiāng sòng yí cì lǐwù.」Wǒ juéde tā shuō de duì, dànshì rúguǒ wǒ qù zhǎo tā tán zhè jiàn shì, tā yídìng huì xiān qǐng wǒ jìnqù hē chá, ránhòu wǒ yòu děi dài yí gè lǐwù qù…… zhè ge xúnhuán shénme shíhou cái néng jiéshù?
text_en: My neighbor across the hall and I have a strange problem — we're both too polite. Every time we meet in the hallway, he says 'you go first,' I say 'no no no, you go first,' and then we stand there yielding to each other for several minutes. Once we yielded so long at the elevator that the doors closed and it left. Last week he brought me a box of mooncakes. The next day I gave him a bag of fruit. Then he gave me a package of tea leaves. Now I feel I need to give him something else, but I don't know what. My wife said 'you two should sign a contract limiting gift exchanges to once a year.' I think she's right, but if I go talk to him about it, he'll definitely invite me in for tea first, and then I'll have to bring a gift… when will this cycle ever end?

questions.q_zh: 他们在楼道里遇到的时候会怎么做？
questions.q_en: What do they do when they meet in the hallway?

options.pinyin: hùxiāng dǎ zhāohu jiù zǒu le
hùxiāng ràng le hǎo jǐ fēnzhōng
options.text_en: yield to each other for minutes
pretend not to see each other

questions.q_zh: 太太建议他们怎么做？
questions.q_en: What does his wife suggest they do?

options.pinyin: qiān yí gè sòng lǐwù de hétong
bān dào bié de dìfang qù

questions.q_zh: 为什么他不敢去找邻居谈这件事？
questions.q_en: Why is he afraid to talk to the neighbor about it?

options.text_en: afraid the neighbor will be angry

options.text: 怕又要开始新一轮送礼
options.pinyin: pà yòu yào kāishǐ xīn yì lún sòng lǐ
options.text_en: afraid it'll start another gift cycle
